content.text:
I now have consistent, well-corroborated data. I have everything needed to report. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited (BSE SME: 544310; CIN U40109GJ2002PLC040833) — Verification Report

**Important sourcing caveat up front:** I downloaded the official DRHP (412 pages, hosted on BSE) but this environment has no PDF text-extraction/rendering capability, so I could **not** read the prospectus internals directly. Items that live only inside the prospectus (the exact customer table, formal ageing schedules, the litigation chapter) are therefore corroborated via secondary sources that quote the company, and I flag each as such. Most-load-bearing numbers (shareholding, FY26 results, cash flow) come from filings mirrored on Screener and the company's own results coverage, cross-checked across two or more sources.

A second hazard worth noting: there is a sister/affiliate company, **Yash Optics & Lens Ltd** (NSE Emerge), which is easy to confuse with this company. I have kept them separate; one search inadvertently surfaced the Optics DRHP on NSE — that is **not** this company.

---

## 1. Listing venue — CONFIRMED: BSE SME (not NSE Emerge)

The shares are listed on the **BSE SME platform**, listed 19 Dec 2024. This is confirmed across multiple independent sources, including a primary-grade outlet: Business Standard's headline literally reads "**BSE SME** Yash Highvoltage's IPO ends with 169 times subscription." Screener and Groww both show exchange = BSE, code 544310, SME segment. The IPO was a book-built SME issue of 75,35,000 shares at ₹146 (face value ₹5), aggregating ~₹110.01 crore, bidding 12–16 Dec 2024.

**Verdict:** Sources claiming NSE Emerge are wrong (likely conflating it with Yash Optics & Lens). It is **BSE SME**. High confidence.

## 2. Promoter shareholding and pledging

- **Promoter holding: 57.94% as of the most recent quarter (Mar 2026)**, essentially flat over the year (Mar 2025: 57.89% → Sep 2025: 57.94% → Mar 2026: 57.94%). Source: Screener (mirroring BSE shareholding filings).
- **Pledging: No promoter pledge is disclosed** in the shareholding pattern on Screener or Groww. I found no filing indicating any pledged promoter shares.
- **Promoter:** Mr. Keyur Girishchandra Shah (corroborated across Chittorgarh-class sources and LinkedIn).

**Verdict:** ~57.94%, no disclosed pledge. Confidence high on the percentage; "no pledge" is high-confidence-but-by-absence (I could not open the BSE shareholding PDF itself to confirm a literal 0.00% pledge line).

## 3. The ~₹150 crore fundraise — approved as a ceiling, instrument not yet fixed

- The board met **13 May 2026** to consider FY26 results and "fund raising of up to ₹150 crore."
- **Shareholder approval for up to ₹150 crore has been obtained**, but management indicated it may not use the full limit — it is **targeting ~₹100–110 crore** (per the FY26 earnings-call coverage, multibagg.ai).
- **Instrument (QIP / preferential / rights) is NOT yet specified/finalised** in any source I could reach — the resolutions are framed as an enabling ceiling.
- **Intended use:** primarily **capex** — the greenfield/expansion RIP-bushing facility at Vadodara, extending product range up to **550 kV** and strengthening testing infrastructure. Some early coverage also framed it loosely as "growth and debt repayment," but the more detailed earnings-call coverage emphasises capex; note the company runs low leverage (debt/equity ~0.17x), so debt repayment is a minor element at most.

**Verdict:** Approved as a ceiling, not a committed/closed raise; instrument undetermined. I could NOT verify from a primary filing (postal-ballot/EGM result PDF) the exact resolution wording or whether it specifies QIP vs preferential — flag this as unverified at the primary level.

## 4. FY26 audited cash flow vs profit — CFO well below PAT

- **Net profit (PAT), FY26 (consolidated, YE 31 Mar 2026): ₹37.34 crore** (₹374 million), up ~75% YoY. Revenue ₹235.16 cr (+57%), EBITDA ₹60.4 cr (margin ~25.7%), EPS ₹13.08.
- **Cash flow from operations (CFO): ₹8.82 crore** (stated as "₹882.02 Lakhs," corroborated independently by Screener's "~₹9 cr" operating cash flow line).
- **CFO/PAT ≈ 0.24 (roughly 24%).** Operating cash conversion is weak — CFO is far below reported profit.
- Screener also shows FY26 **investing cash flow ≈ −₹15 cr** and **financing ≈ +₹1 cr**.

**Why the gap (corroborating evidence):** working capital ballooned with the 57% revenue surge — inventory roughly doubled and receivables rose (see point 5). This is the proximate cause of the low CFO/PAT.

**Verdict:** CFO ≈ ₹8.8 cr vs PAT ₹37.3 cr — a material divergence. Confidence high (two independent sources agree on ~₹9 cr / ₹882 lakh).

## 5. Receivables and inventory — sharp working-capital build; formal ageing NOT verified

- **Inventory: ₹66.65 crore (FY26) vs ₹29.67 crore (FY25)** — more than doubled.
- **Trade receivables: ₹30.57 crore (FY26) vs ₹27.73 crore (FY25)** — modest rise.
- The inventory surge is consistent with the order book climbing to ~₹400 cr (from ₹150 cr a year earlier) and the expansion build-out, and explains most of the CFO shortfall.

**Verdict:** I could NOT obtain the formal **ageing schedules** (the bucketed <6m / 6m–1y / >1y tables from the notes/annual report) from any primary source I could open. Only the headline balances are verified. Flag the ageing breakdown as **unverified**.

## 6. Customer list — partially corroborated; aggregator claims are a mix of accurate and unverified

What I can support from company-attributable sources:
- **Global MNC OEMs:** The company (via a transformer-technology.com profile quoting it) names "**Siemens, GE, Hitachi-ABB, Toshiba, CG, Schneider, Hyundai, BTW, and many more**." This supports the aggregator claims of **Hitachi and GE** (and Siemens), framed as OEM customers.
- **Indian utilities:** The FY26/FY25 directors' report references "premier central Utilities such as **POWERGRID & NTPC**," and notes its dry-type RIP bushings are embedded in POWERGRID/NTPC technical specifications and the CEA standard spec. This supports **NTPC and POWERGRID** — but as utilities whose specs drive demand / as approved-vendor relationships, not necessarily as direct purchasers.
- A company blog lists older domestic customers (Voltamp Transformers Vadodara, Crompton Greaves, EMCO, AREVA, MSEB, NEEPCO, Vijai Electricals, etc.) — this supports **Voltamp**, but the blog is dated (2009-era) and not the prospectus.
- **TARIL (Transformers & Rectifiers India) and Atlanta Electricals:** I found **no company-attributable confirmation** that these are customers. In the sources I read they appear only as **peer/comparable companies** in the transformer space, not as named Yash customers. The aggregator's pairing of them with Yash's customer list looks like a likely conflation.

**Verdict:**
- Supported as customers/partners: Siemens, GE, Hitachi(-ABB), Toshiba, CG, Schneider, Hyundai; and NTPC/POWERGRID (utility/spec relationships); Voltamp (historical).
- **NOT verified as customers: TARIL and Atlanta Electricals** — treat the aggregator claim with skepticism; they read as peers, not clients.
- The **exact prospectus customer table** I could not read (DRHP unreadable here), so I cannot confirm precisely which names the RHP itself lists as material customers. Flag as partially unverified.

## 7. SEBI actions, auditor qualifications, material litigation — none found

- **Auditor opinion FY26: UNMODIFIED (clean).** Per BSE-filing coverage on Screener: "Auditor issued unmodified opinion for FY26 results" (17 May 2026). The FY25 directors' report similarly records the statutory auditor's report with no reservations and the secretarial audit "without any qualification, reservation, adverse remark or disclaimer."
- **Material litigation / regulatory orders: none disclosed.** FY25 directors' report states "no significant and material order was passed by the Regulators or courts."
- **SEBI actions against company/promoter:** I found **no** SEBI order, investigation, or enforcement action against Yash Highvoltage or Mr. Keyur Shah in any source searched.
- Note (not adverse): the company completed a **₹5.24 cr acquisition of a 50% stake in Sukrut Electric Company** (drives the consolidated FY26 reporting). This is corporate activity, not litigation.

**Verdict:** No auditor qualifications, no material litigation, no SEBI action found. Confidence moderate-to-high — this is partly an "absence of evidence" finding; I could not open the prospectus litigation chapter or SEBI's order database directly, so I cannot rule out minor/ordinary-course matters disclosed inside the DRHP.

---

## What I could NOT verify from primary sources (flagged)
1. **Prospectus internals** — the DRHP PDF was downloaded (`...\tool-results\webfetch-1781335969600-lg0fi2.pdf`, 7.3 MB, 412 pp.) but is **unreadable in this environment** (no PDF text extraction). So the exact customer table, the litigation section, and ageing schedules from the RHP itself are not directly confirmed.
2. **Fundraise instrument** (QIP vs preferential vs rights) and the **primary resolution wording** — only secondary coverage; I could not open the BSE board-outcome/postal-ballot filing.
3. **Receivables/inventory ageing buckets** — only headline balances confirmed.
4. **Pledge = 0** and **SEBI/litigation = nil** are partly absence-of-evidence (the underlying BSE shareholding PDF and SEBI order database were not directly opened).

A reader who needs the prospectus-internal items confirmed should open the DRHP directly at the BSE link below, and pull the BSE board-meeting outcome dated ~13–15 May 2026 for the fundraise resolution wording.
